title: Dark chocolate bark with pistachios and cranberries
subtitle: Thin-poured dark chocolate set with nuts and dried fruit on top
excerpt: Chocolate bark is melted dark chocolate poured thin onto a lined tray, scattered with toppings, and left to set. No tempering required: the bark is thin enough that the texture is good even from untempered chocolate. A handful of pistachios and dried cranberries give colour and crunch.
sourceNotes: Chocolate set with dried fruit and nuts as a slab confection is documented in domestic confectionery guides from the twentieth century. The use of dark chocolate as a flat confection poured onto a tray is a standard domestic preparation requiring no specialised technique beyond melting chocolate. The combination of dark chocolate with nuts and dried fruit is a public-domain confectionery tradition appearing in domestic baking references worldwide. No proprietary method or recipe is implicated in this tutorial.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale directly. Use a second lined tray for a larger batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in a cool place for up to 2 weeks. Refrigerating causes bloom (grey surface) but does not affect flavour.

glossaryTerms.definition: Chocolate bloom is the grey-white surface haze that appears when chocolate is stored in the fridge or in a warm environment. There are two kinds: fat bloom (cocoa butter migrating to the surface as the chocolate re-solidifies) and sugar bloom (moisture dissolving and recrystallising the sugar on the surface). Neither affects flavour; both affect appearance. Bark kept at cool room temperature (around 18°C) without temperature fluctuations will stay glossy. Refrigerating is fine if necessary but produces a dull finish.

content.text: Chocolate bark is one of the fastest homemade sweets. Ten minutes of work, an hour of setting time. The toppings are flexible: sea salt flakes, any nut, any dried fruit, freeze-dried berries, cocoa nibs. The pistachios and cranberries combination is colour-led — vivid green and red on dark chocolate.

Set a heatproof metal bowl over a pan of barely simmering water (the base of the bowl should not touch the water). Add the {{dark-chocolate}} and leave until mostly melted; stir until smooth. Remove from the heat.

Pour the melted chocolate onto a baking tray lined with baking paper. Spread to about 4 mm thickness with the icing spatula — not too thin, or it will be fragile. Scatter the {{pistachios}}, {{dried-cranberries}}, and {{sea-salt-flakes}} (if using) evenly over the surface. Press the toppings in very gently with the flat of your hand.
Leave at cool room temperature to set fully, at least 1 hour. The chocolate is ready when it snaps cleanly rather than bending. See the
note if you need to refrigerate. Break into irregular pieces and store in a cool, dry place.
